questions.q: Which office did Charan Singh hold at the Union level in 1979-1980?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh served as Prime Minister of India from July 1979 to January 1980.

questions.q: Charan Singh is popularly remembered as a champion of which section of society?
questions.explanation: He is widely known for his focus on rural and agrarian issues and is called a champion of the farmer.

questions.q: Charan Singh's birthday, 23 December, is observed in India as which day?
questions.explanation: His birthday, 23 December, is observed as Kisan Diwas in honour of his focus on agrarian issues.

questions.q: In which year did Charan Singh become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh became Prime Minister in July 1979.

questions.q: Charan Singh's tenure as Prime Minister is best described as which of the following?
options: A short tenure of a few months
questions.explanation: His tenure as Prime Minister lasted only from July 1979 to January 1980, a few months.

questions.q: Charan Singh's political career is most closely associated with which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh was a leading political figure from Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Before becoming Prime Minister, Charan Singh had served as the head of government of which state?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh served as Chief Minister of Uttar Pradesh before his national premiership.

questions.q: Which theme dominated Charan Singh's political agenda throughout his career?
questions.explanation: His agenda was centred on rural and agrarian development and the welfare of farmers.

questions.q: Kisan Diwas, associated with Charan Singh, is observed on which date each year?
questions.explanation: Kisan Diwas is observed on 23 December, Charan Singh's birthday.

questions.q: What kind of national office was the post Charan Singh held in 1979?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of the Union government, the office Charan Singh held in 1979.

questions.q: Charan Singh's term as Prime Minister ended in which month and year?
questions.explanation: His term as Prime Minister ended in January 1980.

questions.q: Charan Singh became Prime Minister in 1979 immediately after which Prime Minister's tenure?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh succeeded Morarji Desai, who headed the earlier Janata Party government.

questions.q: Charan Singh's premiership fell within which broader political phase in India?
options: The period following the 1977 Janata experiment
questions.explanation: His brief premiership came in the period following the Janata Party experiment of 1977.

questions.q: Which subject area is Charan Singh most identified with in his writings and policy work?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh wrote and worked extensively on the agrarian economy and questions of land.

questions.q: Charan Singh became Prime Minister with support arrangements typical of which kind of politics?
questions.explanation: His government depended on coalition and outside-support arrangements rather than a single-party majority.

questions.q: The day commemorated on Charan Singh's birthday primarily honours which group?
questions.explanation: Kisan Diwas on 23 December honours farmers, reflecting Charan Singh's lifelong focus.

questions.q: Charan Singh's rise to national prominence is rooted in his long career in which arena?
questions.explanation: He built his national standing through a long career in state and national politics.

questions.q: Which description best fits Charan Singh's standing in Indian political history?
options: A Prime Minister with a brief tenure known for agrarian focus
questions.explanation: He is remembered as a Prime Minister of brief tenure known above all for his agrarian focus.

questions.q: In the office Charan Singh held in 1979, who formally appoints the Prime Minister?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is appointed by the President of India, the office Charan Singh held in 1979.

questions.q: Charan Singh's premiership is counted among which category of Indian Prime Ministers?
options: Those who never sat in Parliament
Those with short tenures during the late 1970s
questions.explanation: He is counted among Prime Ministers with short tenures during the political churn of the late 1970s.

questions.q: Which slogan-like idea is most consistent with Charan Singh's political emphasis?
options: Priority to rural and farmer welfare
Priority to heavy industry above all
questions.explanation: His political emphasis centred on rural and farmer welfare above competing priorities.

questions.q: Charan Singh held the office of Prime Minister for approximately how long?
questions.explanation: He held the office of Prime Minister for roughly six months, from July 1979 to January 1980.

questions.q: Within India's order of Prime Ministers, Charan Singh served immediately before which Prime Minister returned to office in 1980?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi returned as Prime Minister in 1980, following Charan Singh's brief tenure.

questions.q: Charan Singh is often described as which sequential Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh is counted as the fifth Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: Charan Singh's premiership began in the same year as which of the following?
options: The 1979 collapse of the earlier Janata government
questions.explanation: His premiership began in 1979, following the collapse of the earlier Janata Party government.

questions.q: Charan Singh's identification with the farmer is institutionalised in India chiefly through which observance?
options: A national farmers' day on his birthday
A public holiday on Republic Day
questions.explanation: His identification with farmers is institutionalised through Kisan Diwas, observed on his birthday.

questions.q: Charan Singh's career before 1979 placed him prominently in the politics of which region of India?
questions.explanation: His career was rooted in the politics of the northern Hindi-speaking belt, especially Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Which statement about Charan Singh's national office is constitutionally accurate?
options: He was the constitutional head of state
He presided over the Rajya Sabha
He was the chief judicial authority
He headed the Union executive as leader of the Council of Ministers
questions.explanation: As Prime Minister he headed the Union executive as leader of the Council of Ministers.

questions.q: Charan Singh's tenure is notable in Indian constitutional history as an example of what?
options: A short-lived government in a period of political instability
questions.explanation: His tenure illustrates a short-lived government during a period of political instability.

questions.q: The legacy most consistently attached to Charan Singh in civic memory is his association with which cause?
options: The cause of urban industrial expansion
The cause of the Indian farmer
questions.explanation: In civic memory he is most consistently associated with the cause of the Indian farmer.
